Um, we've had feedback
that he's now been promoted into a senior role
and giving back to other Pacific needs
that are not in employment education and training.
So, um, at the heart of this program's funding, it's,
it's not just about about creating, you know, this is one
of two 30 in 36 place.
One, one more step on on this, um, because
although I appreciate each individual case,
I guess we are dealing on mass with an unemployment program,
uh, problem for Pacific at the moment.
Um, I'm just noting the committees 2020 3 24
report, uh, where you intended
to get at least 7,500 pasifica people
who are not in employment educational training
into placements between June 21
and June 25 as part of the PU program.
Now that program's been cut by $22 million.
Does the ministry have a similar target for 2025
And if so, what is it?
Um, we, the program is the program
that we'll be transitioning
to the Ministry for Social Development.
So our role will be continuing to work with the ministry
to address, um, issues of unemployment
and to ensure that they're supported in delivering
to Pacific communities.
Will they have a similar target to
what you had set for the,
We're going to continue to work with, um, the ministry
around what that might look like.
I know that they will be proactively looking at this, um,
to address it in the wider context.
Um, so we'll, we'll, we'll go through
that process at the appropriate time so
that the program will transition at the end
of this financial year.
And we've started those conversations, now
We're getting closer time.
And I've just got a question that's, um,
let's take a slightly different tack.
But obviously, um, the work you do is in policy work
around Pacific people in New Zealand is really important.
Another po uh, thing
that's really important is the connection
to the wider diaspora, um, uh, across the Pacific and, and,
and New Zealand's connection,
which is a very strong one, one.
Uh, so do you provide advice, uh,
and any support et cetera on
that, that you can speak to us about?
Sure. We, uh, and is that
Reflected in your report, Geraldine?
'cause I was just looking for the impact
connections in conduct.
Um, I, I'm not, yes, it is, uh, reflected in the report.
I, I think firstly, um,
we support the minister
and the Ministry for Foreign Affairs, uh,
with work in the diaspora, uh, sorry, in the region.
And, uh, particularly the diaspora have really strong
connections into the region.
Our languages program now, for example,
we have more language speakers of, and Maori
and Newan here in New Zealand.
So New Zealand plays a really important, um,
role in the Pacific
and internationally in preserving, uh, culture and identity.
So the team have been working really hard on that.
Quick stop. Um, sort of following on from what Joseph
and Helen was said as well, about the advice that you've,
you've provided to, to the Prime Minister for example,
around exactly that, the importance of those connections
between here and over the Diabo communities.
We have the treaty app friendship with Samo
Rowan countries as well.
It seems to me to be a very good part of an argument
why we should retain and keep
and resource better at the ministry Pacific peoples.
And I'm wondering if that advice is flowing through
to relevant ministries.
I I don't have any further comment
to make on that. Thank you for your
Comment.
Thank, thank you very much for your time this morning
for your work with very important
and valued for civic people and across the wider region.
Thank you.
